originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&flags` is never used.

presenceCondition: (and (= CROSS_COMPILE false) (= WIN32 false) (= NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES true))
model:
(define-fun WIN32 () Bool
  false)
(define-fun NEED_ENHANCED_ESCAPES () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CROSS_COMPILE () Bool
  false)
reasoning: Line 38: `flags |= T_ESCAPE_SHELL_CMD` inside the NEED_ENHANCED block. flags is set at L21 (flags=0) and potentially modified at L38, but is never read — the loop continues and overwrites it, or function returns. Dead store. No '// ERROR' comment → new bug not in VBDB. CROSS_COMPILE=F is needed to avoid the contradiction (CROSS_COMPILE=T prevents NEED_ENHANCED from being defined). However WIN32=F is unnecessary: the dead store at L38 occurs whenever NEED_ENHANCED=T, whether WIN32 is T or F (WIN32 only determines if NEED_ENHANCED is internally defined, not whether the dead store exists). PC is over-constrained by WIN32=F. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
